title: 2025_canadian_federal_election
background: I am a statistician who monitors and analyzes federal elections in Canada, Mexico, and the United States. Following major federal elections in these countries, I am generally responsible for compiling and verifying detailed statistics for each party involved in the election. These statistics are mostly used as additional data for building models to forecast future elections; my job does not involve making political or policy commentary on the basis of these results, and it's not my area of expertise anyway. Since the audience for my work is generally other statisticians and technical people within my department, the written work I produce tends to be fairly technical.
query: Help me compile parliamentary and vote share statistics on the 2025 Canadian Federal elections. This should include how many seats each party won or lost, how many total seats each party now has, any information on total (popular) vote share available for the major parties and for any specific candidates for which it is available. Any demographic breakdowns of support for particular parties or candidates would also be helpful. For all statistics, please also include as much detail as possible on their source, as this helps me get a sense for how credible they are.

title: 2025_canadian_federal_election
background: In my role at Elections Canada, I analyze federal election outcomes to understand voting behavior, regional shifts, and the performance of Canada’s electoral system. I contribute to post-election analytical briefs and longer-term reports used by senior officials, parliamentary committees, and other federal departments. My work focuses on turnout, vote distribution across ridings, party performance, minority vs. majority outcomes, and how external political factors influence Canadian elections. These analyses are non-partisan and intended primarily for policymakers and institutional researchers.
query: I am looking to understand the key dynamics and outcomes of the 2025 Canadian Federal Election. Specifically, I am interested in how vote shares translated into seat outcomes under the first-past-the-post system, the regional patterns that enabled the Liberals to form government, and the factors contributing to the sharp decline in NDP support. Information on polling trends during the campaign, the role of leadership changes, and the influence of external political pressures—such as U.S. political rhetoric—would be particularly valuable. I am less focused on individual campaign messaging and more on structural, regional, and electoral-system-level explanations for the result.

title: 2025_Myanmar_earthquake
persona_title: Analyst for the Myanmar Department of Agriculture
background: In my job at the Department of Agriculture, I track crop yields for various major Burmese crops, including rice, beans, pulses, and oilseeds—specializing in rice. I contribute to monthly and annual reports for the Department on total production for these crops and to forecasts for near-term future yields. Occasionally, I also work on division-specific reports. These reports are generally public and are mostly read by policymakers and analysts within other departments in the Myanmar government.
query: I am trying to understand the impact of the 2025 Myanmar Earthquake on agricultural and farm land throughout the country. Any information on the regions affected, the total acreage rendered infertile, and any crops likely to be impacted is valuable. I am less interested in the impact on livestock.

title: 2025_Myanmar_earthquake
background: I analyze major seismic events across Southeast Asia, with a focus on the tectonic setting, fault mechanics, and ground-motion characteristics that drive earthquake impacts. My work involves examining regional plate boundaries, active fault systems, historical seismicity, and instrumental records to explain why earthquakes occur and how energy is released and propagated. I also assess post-event impact data—including shaking intensity reports, damage assessments, and geospatial analyses—to evaluate the geographic extent and severity of destruction across affected regions. The reports I produce are intended for technical and analytical audiences and emphasize geological mechanisms, observational evidence, and source credibility rather than emergency response or policy considerations.
query: I need a report on the 2025 earthquake in Myanmar. The report should provide geological context about why and how the earthquake occurred and help its audience understand the extent of the earthquake’s impact, both in terms of regions affected and severity of destruction across those regions. The report should incorporate both high-level information about the earthquake and details from primary sources.

title: Blue_Mission_Ghost_1
background: I am interested in the policy and organizational structure of modern space exploration, particularly how commercial and institutional actors work together. I am aware that lunar missions are shifting from government-led programs to commercially provided services, and I want to understand how these public–private collaborations are structured and who is responsible for different mission elements.
query: I need a report on Blue Ghost Mission 1 that describes who was involved, how the spacecraft traveled from Earth to the Moon, what occurred during the landing, and what activities took place after touchdown. It should also explain the broader significance of the mission in the context of lunar exploration, especially compared to earlier Moon landings and other recent commercial efforts.

title: Blue_Mission_Ghost_1
persona_title: Lunar Science Program Analyst for NASA’s Commercial Lunar Payload Services (CLPS)
background: In my role supporting NASA’s CLPS initiative, I analyze commercial lunar missions that deliver scientific payloads to the Moon. I focus on mission design, launch and transit profiles, landing performance, payload integration, and early surface operations. My work contributes to internal NASA assessments, inter-agency coordination documents, and post-mission summaries that evaluate scientific return, technical risk, and commercial partner performance. These reports are primarily read by NASA program managers, partner agencies, and government science and technology stakeholders.
query: I am trying to assess the performance and scientific outcomes of Firefly Aerospace’s Blue Ghost Mission 1 as part of NASA’s Commercial Lunar Payload Services program. Relevant information includes the mission’s launch and transit profile, lunar orbit and descent operations, landing site selection, and the execution of powered descent and hazard avoidance. I am particularly interested in the status and operation of NASA scientific payloads delivered to the lunar surface, the data returned during transit and surface operations, and how the mission advances understanding of lunar geology, the Moon’s interior, and the interaction between the lunar environment and Earth’s magnetosphere. Information on mission milestones, surface conditions, and early scientific results is especially valuable.

title: Liberation-Day-tariffs
background: I run a business that imports goods into the United States, so changes in trade policy can quickly affect what I pay, how I source products, and what I charge customers. I pay close attention to economic and political news to keep up with these changes, even though I don’t have formal training in trade law or economics. What I care most about is how tariffs are put in place and whether they’re likely to stick.
query: I’m trying to understand the 2025 “Liberation Day” tariffs announced by Donald Trump so I can assess how they might affect businesses in the U.S. that rely on imported goods. I want to know what the tariffs involved, how they were announced and rolled out, and how courts and markets reacted in practice. I’m especially interested in what happened after the initial announcement, including any legal challenges or uncertainty that could affect whether these tariffs remain in place. I’m generally less interested in the political messaging around the tariffs or in broader ideological debates about trade policy, except where they help explain how stable or predictable these measures are likely to be for businesses.

title: Liberation-Day-tariffs
persona_title: Senior Trade Policy Analyst at a U.S. Economic Policy Think Tank
background: I work as a senior analyst at a DC based economic policy think tank focusing on U.S. trade law and international economic policy. My work involves evaluating the legality, economic effects, and geopolitical consequences of U.S. trade actions, including tariffs imposed under emergency authorities. I regularly brief congressional staff, publish policy memos, and contribute expert commentary on how court rulings affect executive authority, global markets, and ongoing trade negotiations. My audience includes policymakers, legal scholars, and international trade analysts.
query: I am analyzing the recent U.S. Court of International Trade decision blocking President Trump’s “Liberation Day” tariffs. I want to understand the legal reasoning behind the injunction, including how the court interpreted the International Emergency Economic Powers Act (IEEPA) and Congress’s constitutional authority over tariffs. I am also interested in the categories of tariffs affected, the expected appeals process, and the potential implications for U.S. trade negotiations and financial markets. Information on reactions from the White House, legal experts, and market responses would be especially useful.

title: 2025-Central-Texas-floods
background: I analyze extreme rainfall and flooding events across the southern United States, with a focus on diagnosing the atmospheric conditions that lead to high-impact floods. My work involves examining synoptic- and mesoscale circulation patterns, moisture transport, convective organization, and their persistence over multi-day periods. I routinely use reanalysis datasets, satellite and radar observations, and numerical weather prediction outputs to reconstruct how extreme precipitation events develop and why they produce elevated flood risk. My analyses are intended for technical audiences and emphasize physical mechanisms and empirical evidence rather than forecasting operations or policy implications.
query: I need a report explaining the atmospheric conditions that led to the Central Texas floods in July 2025, especially how various weather systems and circulation patterns combined to produce extreme rainfall. The report should also describe how these features developed and persisted in the days leading up to the event, and why they created such a high risk for widespread flooding.

title: 2025-Alaska-typhoon
background: I am a civil engineer for the U.S. National Oceanic and Atmospheric Association (NOAA) working specifically within the National Ocean Service (NOS). I study the effectiveness of various forms of municipal infrastructure (e.g. levees, floodwalls, retention basins) and building standards (e.g. for impact-resistant windows, reinforced roofs) at mitigating damage from ocean-related weather events (hurricanes, tsunamis, coastal flooding). I am interested both in economic and humanitarian damage and while I tend to focus on data from natural disasters, I am also sometimes tasked with projects focused on longer-term issues, such as problems caused by rising sea levels and coastal erosion, and how to better design city infrastructure to combat these problems.
query: I am investigating the 2025 Alaska Typhoon as part of a broader project on recommendations for building code reform for housing in coastal cities in the American Northwest (including Alaska). I want to understand: (1) the extent of the damage to housing caused by the typhoon; (2) details on the houses that were damaged (where they were located, how they were damaged, what they were made of); and (3) any speculation on why these homes were not more resilient to the storm. Apartments and condos are also in scope, but I am generally less interested in non-residential infrastructure except insofar as it may be informative for my project. I am particularly interested in any information on the above points that is likely to also be relevant to recommendations for cities in Pacific NW states much further south (Washington, Oregon).

title: 2025-Alaska-typhoon
background: I am a government official in Alaska responsible for natural disaster relief. I need to follow developments of natural disasters to better inform my policy decisions and make real time decisions on how and where to allocate disaster relief.
query: I need a report on the Oct. 2025 Typhoon in Alaska. The report should include a general overview of the event, but I am most interested in knowing about damage caused by the typhoon, impacts on residents, information on fatalities and missing persons, emergency response, and the situation of government aid.

title: 2025_nepal_youth_protests
persona_title: Spokesperson for the Office of Municipal Executive (Kathmandu Metropolitan City)
background: As Spokesperson for the Office of Municipal Executive in Kathmandu, I am responsible for all public-facing communications from the Office and act as a mouthpiece for the Mayor of Kathmandu and his administration. I regularly prepare and deliver press releases on major events in the city and issues affecting it, and I must always be ready to answer questions from reporters. I also prepare briefings to be distributed on our website and various mailing lists, and often deliver speeches on behalf of the Mayor at ceremonies, festivals, and other important gatherings in the city.
query: In the wake of recent (2025) youth protests throughout Nepal, I need to prepare a briefing on the extent of damage to municipal infrastructure in Kathmandu resulting from these protests. I want to know which buildings have been damaged, how they have been damaged, and any information on potential suspects and their motives. I am not especially interested in damage that may have occurred outside city limits. The report need not be exactly in the format of a press briefing, but should be able to be easily edited into one.

title: 2025_nepal_youth_protests
persona_title: Freelance Investigative Journalist Covering South Asian Politics and Civil Movements
background: I report on political developments, protests, and social movements across South Asia, focusing on grassroots mobilizations, youth activism, and government responses. My work involves analyzing firsthand footage, social media posts, and news reports to provide in-depth articles for international media outlets. I am particularly interested in understanding how protests unfold on the ground, the scale of public participation, and the societal and political consequences.
query: I am investigating the 2025 youth-led protests in Kathmandu, Nepal, as part of a broader project on political mobilization and civil unrest in South Asia. Relevant information includes the locations and key targets of protests, the size, composition, and activities of protest crowds, and any resulting effects. I am particularly interested in the interactions between protestors, police, and military forces, including any evacuations, interventions, or clashes; the political outcomes, including resignations or policy changes; and any damage to infrastructure or property.

title: 2025_Palisades_fires
background: I work in property risk assessment for an insurance company, focusing on how natural hazards affect high-value residential areas. A lot of my work involves looking at what actually gets damaged after major events and using that information to guide underwriting and pricing decisions. I’m especially interested in how factors like location, density, and local conditions influence how sever losses end up being.
query: I’m looking at the 2025 Palisades Fire to get a clearer picture of property risk in the surrounding neighborhoods. I want to know which areas were hit hardest, how quickly the fire moved, and what the damage patterns show about which types of properties and streets were most at risk. I’m especially interested in differences between neighborhoods and any clues about why certain homes or corridors suffered more. I’m generally less concerned with day-to-day emergency response or the criminal investigation, except where those details help explain why losses were concentrated in some areas. My goal is to use what happened here to improve risk estimates and underwriting decisions for similar coastal communities.

title: 2025_Palisades_fires
background: I study the development, spread, and environmental impacts of wildfires in California. My work focuses on understanding how fires ignite, how they progress under different weather and terrain conditions, and how human settlements and vegetation patterns influence fire behavior. I use satellite imagery, heatmaps, ground observations, and simulations to model fire growth and intensity. My research informs predictive models, risk assessments, and mitigation strategies for future wildfire events.
query: I am examining the 2025 Palisades Fire to analyze its ignition, progression, and overall impact on the surrounding environment. I am interested in the timeline of fire development, the geographic spread across neighborhoods and vegetation types, and any factors that influenced how quickly and widely the fire moved. Data from heatmaps, aerial imagery, and first-hand footage showing fire intensity and spread patterns are particularly valuable. I’m also interested in areas where fire behavior was altered by natural or built features, such as hills, roads, or protective walls, and in any evidence of holdover or underground fire activity. I am less concerned with individual criminal investigations or personal stories unless they provide insight into fire initiation or growth.

title: 2025_Tropical_Storm_Wipha
persona_title: Deputy Minister of the Coast Guard Administration of Taiwan
background: As a Deputy Minister in Taiwan’s Coast Guard Administration, I work with the other Deputy Ministers to help oversee the safety and security of the waters around Kinmen, Matsu, Penghu, and the main Island of Taiwan, and of coastal Taiwanese communities. This mission involves setting strategic priorities, determining the allocation of our forces, and directing operations across these regions.
query: The Coast Guard of Taiwan is closely monitoring the progression of Tropical Storm Wipha in order to determine whether some of our smaller ships currently stationed off the south shore of Kinmen should be evacuated. To help inform this decision, I would like a report on the trajectory of the storm, its strength and the sea conditions at different points along that trajectory, as well as any information about damage caused by the storm. Any forecasts of its likely trajectory beyond Guangdong is especially appreciated but sources must be included alongside such forecasts.

title: 2025_Tropical_Storm_Wipha
persona_title: Urban Resilience and Emergency Planning Analyst – Guangdong/Hong Kong
background: I work in the municipal emergency management office, specializing in urban resilience planning for major coastal cities in southern China. My responsibilities include assessing the vulnerability of buildings, public infrastructure, and transportation networks to extreme weather events, compiling post-event damage reports, and advising city authorities on disaster preparedness measures. I also track storm-related disruptions to ports, roads, and utilities, and contribute to public safety alerts and recovery planning.
query: I am compiling a detailed report on the impacts of Typhoon Wipha (July 2025) on urban areas in Guangdong, Hong Kong, Macau, and Hainan. I am looking for information on structural damage to buildings, scaffolding, bridges, and roads, as well as flooding, storm surge, and wind-related hazards. Reports of injuries, fatalities, and disruptions to transportation or utilities are also relevant. Additionally, data on wind speeds, rainfall, and the typhoon’s trajectory that contributed to these impacts would be valuable for improving city preparedness and resilience planning.

title: 2025_Shi_Yongxin_Scandal
background: I am a freelance journalist. I mostly write in-depth profiles about international personalities who may be receiving peripheral coverage in North American news outlets. My work has appeared in prestige publications including the New Yorker and the Wall Street Journal, but I also maintain a substack with a moderate following. My target audience is general consumers of news and social media who may want a deeper dive on current events. Readers follow my work because its sharp commentary and memorable details help them sound clever and well-informed at dinner parties.
query: I am writing a profile on the scandals surrounding Shi Yongxin. I heard about the scandal through social media, but have no prior knowledge about Shi Yongxin or the Shaolin Temple, and I have not seen much coverage yet in English-language news outlets. I need background information about Shi Yongxin and Shaolin Temple, particularly anything that is contextually relevant to the scandal. I also want to understand how the scandal has unfolded, from initial rumors to Shi Yongxin’s arrest. The focus of the report should be on Shi Yongxin as a personality– any textural details that will add interest to his characterization will be helpful.

title: 2025_Shi_Yongxin_Scandal
persona_title: Research Analyst, Institute for Cultural and Social Studies
background: I conduct research on social, cultural, and institutional developments in China, with a focus on how public figures and organizations are represented in media and official records. My work involves collecting and analyzing information from multiple sources over time, identifying trends, and tracking the evolution of events or controversies. I produce reports for academic, policy, and journalistic audiences that summarize key developments, highlight patterns, and provide context for social or institutional changes.
query: I am trying to understand the evolution of the Shi Yongxin controversy from the 2010s through 2025. I am particularly interested in how allegations of financial misconduct, personal scandals, and violations of monastic precepts emerged, evolved, and were addressed over time; the shifting media coverage, including interviews and official statements; and how these events affected the Shaolin Temple’s governance, commercialization, and public image. Any information that highlights changes in accusations, investigations, public perception, or Shi Yongxin’s personal and professional conduct across this time period is especially valuable.
